channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Historical and Geopolitical Perspectives on Western Control of Iranian Oil
item.description: The article explores the historical and ongoing western interest in Iran’s oil, tracing its significance from the early 20th century to the present. Oil was first discovered in Iran in 1908 by British...

item.title: Three men arrested for operating a drug den and selling shabu in Koronadal City
item.description: Agents from the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA) Region 12 successfully arrested three men in Koronadal City, South Cotabato, after they sold P81,600 worth of shabu during a controlled buy op...

item.title: Ogun Customs Command Under Acting Comptroller Afeni Records Major Seizures, Revenue Surge and Export Resumption
item.description: Since Acting Comptroller Oladapo Afeni took charge of the Ogun I Area Command of the Nigeria Customs Service at Idiroko border, the command has intensified anti-smuggling operations. Within six weeks,...

item.title: King Charles inaugurates new continuous coastal path around England
item.description: King Charles has inaugurated a new footpath known as the King Charles III England Coast Path, stretching 2,689 miles around the entire coast of England. According to Natural England, this makes it the...

item.title: US Intelligence Chief Tulsi Gabbard Highlights Potential Future Reach of Pakistan's Long-Range Missiles in 2026 Threat Assessment
item.description: According to the 2026 Annual Threat Assessment presented by US Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard before the Senate Intelligence Committee, Pakistan has been included among nations like R...

item.title: Pakistan Denies Claims of Threatening US with Missile Capabilities
item.description: The Pakistani Foreign Office (FO) has strongly refuted statements made by US Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard regarding Pakistan’s missile capabilities posing a threat to the United Sta...

item.title: Plet Bolipata Opens 'The Time Has Come' Art Exhibit
item.description: Artist Plet Bolipata recently launched her latest exhibit titled 'The Time Has Come,' showcasing her distinct style and artistic vision. The opening event featured a gathering of art enthusiasts, crit...

item.title: Val Kilmer to Appear Posthumously in Film Using AI Technology
item.description: Val Kilmer, the iconic actor known for roles in Top Gun, Batman Forever, and The Doors, will appear posthumously in the upcoming action-adventure film As Deep As The Grave, a year after his death from...

item.title: Central Ruet-e-Hilal Committee meets in Islamabad to sight Shawwal crescent for Eid ul Fitr 2026
item.description: The Central Ruet-e-Hilal Committee (RHC) meeting is currently underway in Islamabad under the chairmanship of Maulana Abdul Khabir Azad to sight the Shawwal moon, which will determine the end of Ramad...

item.title: Top 25 Travel Experiences Across Oceania in 2026
item.description: Oceania is shaping up as a top travel destination for 2026, offering a blend of luxury, natural wonders, and cultural immersion. Australia, New Zealand, and South Pacific islands provide travellers wi...

item.title: The Importance of Winning Power Over Moral Victories in Politics
item.description: In this opinion piece, Anthony Koch argues that political success is defined not by moral or ideological correctness, but by the practical ability to govern. He criticizes modern conservatives for pri...

item.title: Eskom staff raise concerns over alleged favouritism in security procurement
item.description: Fresh concerns have emerged at Eskom as employees allege that a senior manager in the security division, Remone Govender, is showing favouritism towards companies he is connected to, specifically Pro ...

item.title: Federal Reserve Raises Inflation Forecast Amid Oil Price Surge Driven by Iran Conflict
item.description: The US Federal Reserve has maintained its current interest rates despite mounting inflationary pressures stemming from the ongoing war with Iran, initiated by US and Israeli military actions at the en...

item.title: Lindi Region Plants Over One Million Trees During Forestry Day Celebrations
item.description: In Lindi Region, the Ministry of Natural Resources and Tourism has reported the planting of 1.2 million trees to mark World Forestry Day and the National Tree Planting Campaign. Dr Ashatu Kijaji, the ...

item.title: Wife Calmly Talks to Husband’s Side Chick, Shares Painful Confession
item.description: A woman named Abigail, whose husband Peter was having an affair, calmly confronted his side chick, Judith, over a phone call that was later shared on TikTok. Instead of yelling or insulting, Abigail e...

item.title: Odisha suspends four officials over negligence after hospital fire kills 12
item.description: In Odisha's Cuttack, a tragic fire broke out in the trauma care intensive care unit of the Srirama Chandra Bhanja Medical College and Hospital early on Monday, resulting in the death of 12 patients. T...

item.title: 67-Year-Old Astrologer Arrested for Allegedly Repeatedly Raping Woman
item.description: Ashok Karat Captain, a 67-year-old well-known astrologer, has been taken into police custody following serious allegations of sexual assault. According to reports, he is accused of repeatedly raping a...

item.title: Worker killed as 22-storey construction site partially collapses in Westlands, Nairobi
item.description: A construction site in Nairobi’s Westlands area turned tragic when the upper section of a 22‑storey building under construction partially collapsed at night, killing one worker and injuring another. E...

item.title: Spider-Man: Brand New Day Trailer Highlights Key Plot Points and Returning Characters
item.description: The first trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day has been released, generating massive attention online. Tom Holland and Zendaya return as Peter Parker and MJ, along with familiar faces from the Marvel...

item.title: Ukrainian Embassy Claims Arrest of Six Citizens in India May Be Politically Motivated and Influenced by Russian Intelligence
